title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Pre-Market Movements
subtitle: March 05, 2026 - Early Trading Session

items.headline: Global Markets Navigate Middle East Conflict; U.S. Futures Mixed
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions stemming from the escalating Middle East conflict continue to heavily influence global financial markets. While Asian equities rebounded sharply, U.S. stock futures are showing mixed signals in early trading, as investors weigh ongoing conflict against better-than-expected U.S. economic data from Wednesday. Oil prices remain elevated, fueling inflation concerns.

items.headline: Impact BioMedical Inc. surges in early trading.
items.summary: Impact BioMedical Inc. leads pre-market gainers.

items.headline: Blue Hat Interactive up significantly.
items.summary: Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology sees strong pre-market gains.

items.headline: Palladyne AI Corp. climbs ahead of market open.
items.summary: Palladyne AI Corp. rose in pre-market, with the company expected to report quarterly loss at $0.18 per share on revenue of $809 thousand.

items.summary: Actelis Networks, Inc. is among the top pre-market losers.

items.summary: Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. shows a significant pre-market decrease.

items.headline: Veeva Systems Inc. soars on strong earnings.
items.summary: Shares of Veeva Systems Inc. gained 11.3% in pre-market trading following upbeat Q4 results and robust FY27 guidance.

items.headline: Broadcom gains after earnings report.
items.summary: Broadcom shares are climbing after its earnings report, with the chip maker noting AI is not disrupting its software business.

items.headline: SES AI Corporation tumbles after Q4 report.
items.summary: SES AI Corporation saw its stock crumble after its Q4 earnings report.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, marked by Iran's missile launches at Israel and earlier strikes by the U.S. and Israel against Iran, continues to rattle global financial markets. The disruption has sent both major oil benchmarks climbing nearly 20% since Friday, raising concerns about a renewed inflation surge.

items.headline: China Sets Economic Growth Target of 4.5%-5% for 2026
items.summary: China has set its economic growth target for 2026 at 4.5% to 5%, according to a government work report submitted on Thursday. Other key targets include urban unemployment around 5.5% and a consumer price index increase of approximately 2%.

items.headline: U.S. Judge Orders $175 Billion Tariff Refunds; New Tariffs Loom
items.summary: A federal judge has ordered the U.S. government to initiate a $175 billion refund process for invalidated Trump tariffs. Meanwhile, President Donald Trump's new 15% global tariff is expected to take effect later this week, potentially impacting trade and consumer costs.

items.headline: Liquidia Reports Strong Q4 2025 Results
items.summary: Liquidia Corporation ($LQDA) announced strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results, reporting $148.3 million in YUTREPIA® sales for 2025 and achieving its second consecutive quarter of profitability.

items.headline: BlackRock Maintains Optimistic Outlook Amidst AI-Driven Earnings
items.summary: BlackRock's March 2026 commentary indicates a cautiously optimistic outlook for the U.S. economy and stock market, driven by 'mega forces' like artificial intelligence. Strategists anticipate a resilient U.S. labor market, which will likely reinforce the Fed's hawkish stance and maintain current interest rates.
